entries.title: 2 Kings 25:1
entries.author: John Wesley (1703–1791)
entries.content: Be it known, &c. - This is a mere fiction, which being confidently affirmed, they thought would easily find belief with a king whose heart and ears they possessed by their hired counsellors.

entries.title: 2 Kings 25:11
entries.author: John Wesley (1703–1791)
entries.content: Darius - Darius the son of Hystaspes, successor of Cambyses.

entries.title: 2 Kings 25:26
entries.author: John Wesley (1703–1791)
entries.content: Now therefore. &c. - If the case had been so fairly stated to Artaxerxes, he would hardly have hindered the work. The people of God could not be persecuted, if they were not belied.
